title: N.H. man accused of forcing woman to marry him, cutting her for satanic ritual
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Daniel Ouellet was arrested Saturday after officers found him with the woman at a New Hampshire Olive Garden.

By Abby Patkin June 9, 2026 | 3:03 PM 3 minutes to read A New Hampshire man was arrested over the weekend after authorities say he threatened a woman into marrying him and cut her hand open in what she described as a “satanic ritual.

” Daniel Ouellet, 47, allegedly reassured police that he was a “really safe guy” after officers found him with the woman at an Olive Garden in Newington, New Hampshire, on Saturday, court documents state.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots’ second-round pick isn’t at mandatory minicamp
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots “He’s not here’s and he’s not under contract at this current time.
" Gabe Jacas has not been present at Patriots minicamp or OTAs.

(AP Photo/CJ Gunther) By Conor Ryan June 9, 2026 | 2:42 PM 2 minutes to read FOXBOROUGH — Speaking ahead of the start of the Patriots’ mandatory minicamp practices, Mike Vrabel acknowledged that every player currently under contract with New England — including Christian Gonzalez — is present and accounted for in Foxborough.

title: As opioid deaths fall to lowest figure in more than a decade, what’s causing the decline?
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Massachusetts recorded 978 opioid-related overdose deaths in 2025, the lowest number since 2012.
More than 22,000 purple flags were planted on Boston Common in 2024, representing lives lost to the opioid epidemic in Massachusetts.

title: Manufacturing plant in Clinton to close, letting go of more than 100 workers
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Business Jabil said the shutdown is part of an effort to optimize operations and align production capacity with business needs.

Angel Gonzalez / AP, File A Clinton manufacturing plant that produces health care products is set to close, leaving 103 employees out of work as global manufacturer Jabil shifts production to other facilities.
The company said the move is part of a broader effort to consolidate operations and better align production capacity with business needs.

title: Healey signs bill extending bar hours to 3 a.m. for World Cup
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Local News Healey said the bill could be a financial boon for the state, which is hosting a confluence of events this summer.
Gov.
Maura Healey on Monday signed a bill to temporarily extend last call and allow cities and towns to create their own public drinking districts this summer.
